frontmatter.title: Glossary
frontmatter.description: Glossary of terms used in Pythia Foundations.

children.value: A separate workspace to make and track changes without impacting other branches of the code
An open-source service that allows users to create sharable, interactive computing environments from
and other repositories. Binder can reproduce a computational environment directly from a
repository, providing a seamless way to share and interact with code and data.
The public service to run Binder is on
. The Binder links on most Project Pythia pages point to
, which is a Pythia-specific implementation of
The underlying technology and infrastructure that powers
. BinderHub deploys and manages the interactive computing environments for

command used to create a local copy of a remote repository.
A snapshot of your Git repository at a specific time.

Conda is an open-source, cross-platform, language-agnostic package manager and environment management system that allows you to quickly install, run, and update packages within your work environment(s). To install
in the context of Project Pythia.
The integration and automation of practices and processes for software development and operations.
An optional feature of GitHub repositories that allows for things like sharing project related announcements, gauging opinions through polls, hosting planning discussions, and Q&A.
A collaborative development workflow where new development takes place on dedicated branches rather than the main branch.
A copy of another project hosted on a collaborative development platform such as GitHub.
A collaborative development workflow where new development takes place on forked repositories rather than the main project repository.
A web platform for collaborative software development.
A common feature of collaborative development platforms such as
used to track bugs, request features, and manage work related for a specific repository.
The Jupyter Notebook software is an open-source web application that allows you to create and share Jupyter Notebooks (

files). Jupyter Notebooks contain executable code, LaTeX equations, visualizations (e.g., plots, pictures), and narrative text. The code does not have to just be Python, other languages such as Julia or R are supported as well. Jupyter Notebooks are celebrated for their interactive output that allows movement between code, code output, explanations, and more code - similar to how scientists think and solve problems. Jupyter Notebooks can be thought of as a living, runnable publication and make for a great presentation platform. See also
Software engines and their environments (e.g., conda environments) that execute the code contained in
A popular web application on which users can create and write their
, as well as explore data, install software, etc. You can find more information on running Jupyter Lab
A web-based platform that authenticates users and launches
applications for users on remote systems.
A free and open-souce operating system based on
Refering to something, such as a
repository, on your computer rather than a remote server.
A specific type of commit that combines changes from two branches.
is a free minimal installer for
package management system; it is a pared-down version of the full Anaconda Python distribution.
Miniforge is the community driven and more permissively licensed minimal installer.

A mechanism to propose code changes to be merged into a repository.
command to upload local commits to a remote repository.

A Python package is a collection of modules, which, in turn, are essentially Python scripts that contain published functionality. There are Python packages for data input, data analysis, data visualization, etc. Each package offers a unique toolset and may have its own unique syntax rules. You can install Python packages with
A storage space containing all of the files and revision history for a software project. Also known as a repo.
A repository hosted on a server such as
The project repository from which a fork was created.
Software tools that track and manage changes to project code and files over time.
